name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَمَا تَأْتِيهِم مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ مِّنْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضِينَ (٤)
```

```english
There did not come to them any sign from among the signs of their Lord, but that they used to disregard it. (4)
```

```arabic
فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ أَنۢبَـٰٓؤُا۟ مَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (٥)
```

```english
They have certainly denied the truth when it came to them, but soon there will come to them the news of what they have been deriding. (5)
```

```arabic
أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن قَرْنٍ مَّكَّنَّـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَا لَمْ نُمَكِّن لَّكُمْ وَأَرْسَلْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ عَلَيْهِم مِّدْرَارًا وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرَ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْنَـٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَنشَأْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْنًا ءَاخَرِينَ (٦)
```

```english
Have they not regarded how many a generation We have destroyed before them whom We had granted power in the land in respects that We did not grant you, and We sent abundant rains for them from the sky and made streams run for them? Then We destroyed them for their sins, and brought forth another generation after them. (6)
```

```arabic
وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ كِتَـٰبًا فِى قِرْطَاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ لَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٧)
```

```english
Had We sent down to you a Book on paper so they could touch it with their [own] hands, [still] the faithless would have said, ‘This is nothing but plain magic.’ (7)
```

```arabic
وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ ۖ وَلَوْ أَنزَلْنَا مَلَكًا لَّقُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ ثُمَّ لَا يُنظَرُونَ (٨)
```

```english
They say, ‘Why has not an angel been sent down to him?’ Were We to send down an angel, the matter would surely be decided, and then they would not be granted any respite. (8)
```

```arabic
وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ مَلَكًا لَّجَعَلْنَـٰهُ رَجُلًا وَلَلَبَسْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّا يَلْبِسُونَ (٩)
```

```english
Had We made him an angel, We would have surely made him a man, and We would have surely confounded them just as they confound [the truth now]. (9)
```

```arabic
وَلَقَدِ ٱسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَحَاقَ بِٱلَّذِينَ سَخِرُوا۟ مِنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (١٠)
```

```english
Apostles were certainly derided before you. Then those who ridiculed them were besieged by what they used to deride. (10)
```

```arabic
قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ ٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (١١)
```

```english
Say, ‘Travel over the land, and then observe how was the fate of the deniers.’ (11)
```

## (Commentary)

The verses point toward their denial of the Truth (الحق) with which the Messenger was sent, their persistence in rejecting the Truth, and their mockery of the signs (آيات) of Allah, the Glorified. This is followed by an exhortation to them, a warning (تخويف), and an admonition (إنذار), as well as a response to some of the nonsensical arguments they raised in denying the manifest Truth.

**His saying, the Exalted:** «وَما تَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ آيَةٍ مِنْ آياتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كانُوا عَنْها مُعْرِضِينَ» — *“There does not come to them any sign from the signs of their Lord but that they are disregardful of it.”* (6:4). This is an indication that the disposition of arrogance (الاستكبار) has become firmly rooted in their souls, resulting in an aversion to the signs that point toward the Truth. Thus, they pay no heed to any sign whatsoever, without distinction between one sign and another, because they have denied the fundamental objective, which is the Truth. This is as He, the Exalted, says: «فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا بِالْحَقِّ لَمَّا جاءَهُمْ» — *“They certainly denied the truth when it came to them.”* (6:5).

**His saying, the Exalted:** «فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ أَنْباءُ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ» — *“Soon there will come to them the news of what they have been deriding.”* (6:5). This is a warning and an admonition, for that which they mock is the Truth, and the Truth must inevitably manifest one day, moving from the realm of report (النبأ) to the realm of direct observation (العيان). He, the Exalted, says: «وَيَمْحُ اللهُ الْباطِلَ وَيُحِقُّ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِماتِهِ» — *“Allah effaces falsehood and confirms the truth by His words.”* (42:24). And He says: «يُرِيدُونَ لِيُطْفِؤُا نُورَ اللهِ بِأَفْواهِهِمْ وَاللهُ مُتِمُّ نُورِهِ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكافِرُونَ. هُوَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُ بِالْهدى وَدِينِ الْحَقِّ لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ» — *“They desire to put out the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah shall perfect His light though the faithless should be averse. It is He who has sent His Apostle with guidance and the true religion that He may make it prevail over all religions, though the polytheists should be averse.”* (61:8-9). And He says in a parable He struck: «كَذلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللهُ الْحَقَّ وَالْباطِلَ فَأَمَّا الزَّبَدُ فَيَذْهَبُ جُفاءً وَأَمَّا ما يَنْفَعُ النَّاسَ فَيَمْكُثُ فِي الْأَرْضِ» — *“As for the scum, it leaves as dross, and as for what profits mankind, it stays on the earth.”* (13:17).

It is well known that when the Truth manifests, the believer (المؤمن) and the disbeliever (الكافر), the humble (الخاضع) and the mocker (المستهزئ), are not equal in how they are affected by it. He, the Exalted, says: «وَلَقَدِ سَبَقَتْ كَلِمَتُنا لِعِبادِنَا الْمُرْسَلِينَ إِنَّهُمْ لَهُمُ الْمَنْصُورُونَ وَإِنَّ جُنْدَنا لَهُمُ الْغالِبُونَ فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّى حِينٍ وَأَبْصِرْهُمْ فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ أَفَبِعَذابِنا يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ فَإِذا نَزَلَ بِساحَتِهِمْ فَساءَ صَباحُ الْمُنْذَرِينَ» — *“Certainly Our decree has gone forth for Our servants, the apostles, that they will indeed be the ones to be helped, and that Our host will indeed be the victors. So leave them alone for a while, and watch them; soon they will see [the truth]. Do they seek to hasten Our punishment? But when it descends in their courtyard, evil will be the morning of those who were warned.”* (37:171-177).

**His saying, the Exalted:** «أَلَمْ يَرَوْا كَمْ أَهْلَكْنا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ» — *“Have they not regarded how many a generation We have destroyed before them...”* (6:6) until the end of the verse. Al-Raghib said: The generation (القرن) refers to a people joined together (المقترنون) in a single period of time, and its plural is *qurun* (قرون).

He also said regarding the saying of the Exalted: «وَأَرْسَلْنَا السَّماءَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِدْراراً» — *“and We sent the sky for them in abundant rains”* (6:6) and «يُرْسِلِ السَّماءَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِدْراراً» — *“He will send for you the sky in abundant rains”* (11:52): Its root is from *al-darr* (الدر) and *al-dirrah* (الدرة), meaning milk. This is used metaphorically for rain, just as the names and attributes of camels are used metaphorically. Thus it is said: "To Allah belongs his milk (لله دره)" [a phrase of praise], and "May your milk be abundant (در درك)." From this is borrowed their saying: "A flowing of its milk (غسوق دره)," meaning its abundance.

In the saying of the Exalted: «مَكَّنَّاهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ما لَمْ نُمَكِّنْ لَكُمْ» — *“whom We had established in the earth as We have not established you”* (6:6), there is a shift (التفات) from the third person (الغيبة) to the second person (الحضور). The apparent reason for this is to remove ambiguity regarding the referent of the pronoun. Had there not been a shift to the second person in His saying: «ما لَمْ نُمَكِّنْ لَكُمْ» — *“as We have not established you”*, the context might have suggested that it referred back to the same group as the pronoun in «مَكَّنَّاهُمْ» — *“whom We had established”*. Otherwise, the original context at the opening of the Surah is in the third person. We have previously discussed the shift occurring in His saying: «هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ» — *“It is He who created you from clay.”* (6:2).

In His saying: «فأَهْلَكْناهُمْ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ» — *“so We destroyed them for their sins”* (6:6), there is an indication that evil deeds and sins (الذنوب) have an influence on general calamities and trials. Regarding this meaning, as well as the influence of good deeds (الحسنات) and acts of obedience (الطاعات) in the outpouring of blessings (النعم) and the descent of benedictions (البركات), there are many verses.

**His saying, the Exalted:** «وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكَ كِتاباً فِي قِرْطاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ» — *“Had We sent down to you a manuscript on parchment and they had touched it with their hands...”* (6:7) until the end of the verse. This is an indication that their arrogance has reached a level where even if We were to send down a book on parchment and they touched it with their hands—such that their senses of sight and hearing were confirmed by the sense of touch—it would be of no use. They would inevitably say at that point: "This is nothing but manifest magic." Therefore, no weight should be given to their nonsensical demand: «وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّى تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنا كِتاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ» — *“and we will not believe your ascension until you bring down to us a book that we may read.”* (17:93).

The word "book" (كتاباً) was used in the indefinite form in His saying: «كِتاباً فِي قِرْطاسٍ» — *“a book on parchment”* because this Book was revealed in a specific manner of revelation (تنزيل) that is only accepted as being revealed in stages (نجوماً) and gradually (تدريجاً). He conditioned it as being "on parchment" (في قرطاس) to be closer to what they suggested, and further from what might occur to their hearts—that the verses revealed to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) were of his own invention without the Trustworthy Spirit (الروح الأمين) bringing them down, as Allah, the Glorified, mentions: «نَزَلَ بِهِ الرُّوحُ الْأَمِينُ عَلى قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُنْذَرِينَ بِلِسانٍ عَرَبِيٍّ مُبِينٍ» — *“The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down to your heart, so that you may be one of the warners, in a clear Arabic language.”* (26:193-195).

**His saying, the Exalted:** «وَقالُوا لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ وَلَوْ أَنْزَلْنا مَلَكاً لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ ثُمَّ لا يُنْظَرُونَ» — *“They say, ‘Why has not an angel been sent down to him?’ Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been decided, and then they would not have been granted any respite.”* (6:8). Their saying «لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ» — *“Why has not an angel been sent down to him?”* is a challenge (تحضيض) intended to express incapacity (التعجيز). The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) had already informed them, through the signs of Allah he recited to them, that what he brought to them was from a noble angel descending from Allah, as in His saying: «إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلُ رَسُولٍ كَرِيمٍ ، ذِي قُوَّةٍ عِنْدَ ذِي الْعَرْشِ مَكِينٍ ، مُطاعٍ ثَمَّ أَمِينٍ» — *“it is indeed the speech of a noble messenger, powerful and established with the Lord of the Throne, obeyed there and trustworthy.”* (81:19-21), among other verses.

Their request for the descent of an angel was for one of two reasons, based on what Allah relates from them in His speech:

**The first:** That the angel should bring them the punishment the Prophet promised them, as He, the Exalted, says: «فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا فَقُلْ أَنْذَرْتُكُمْ صاعِقَةً مِثْلَ صاعِقَةِ عادٍ وَثَمُودَ» — *“But if they turn away, say, ‘I warn you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt of Ad and Thamud.’”* (41:13). And He said: «قُلْ هُوَ نَبَأٌ عَظِيمٌ» — *“Say, ‘It is a great news’”* until He said: «إِنْ يُوحى إِلَيَّ إِلَّا أَنَّما أَنَا نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ» — *“Nothing is revealed to me except that I am a manifest warner.”* (38:67-70).

Since the descent of an angel constitutes a transformation of the Unseen (الغيب) — *that which lies beyond sensory perception and empirical knowledge* — into the manifest (الشهادة), and there is no further goal beyond it, it would result—if they did not believe, and they would never believe due to the entrenched nature of arrogance within them—in the matter being decided between them with justice. At that point, there would be no escape from their destruction, as He, the Exalted, says: «وَلَوْ أَنْزَلْنا مَلَكاً لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ ثُمَّ لا يُنْظَرُونَ» — *“Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been decided, and then they would not have been granted any respite.”* (6:8).

Furthermore, the souls of people deeply immersed in the material world (عالم المادة) and residing in the realm of nature (دار الطبيعة) cannot endure the sight of angels if they were to descend upon them and mingle with them, because their sphere of existence (ظرف) is different. If people were to enter the angels' sphere, it would only be by their transition from the depths of matter to the peak of what lies beyond it, which is death. As He, the Exalted, says: «وَقالَ الَّذِينَ لا يَرْجُونَ لِقاءَنا لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْنَا الْمَلائِكَةُ أَوْ نَرى رَبَّنا لَقَدِ اسْتَكْبَرُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَعَتَوْا عُتُوًّا كَبِيراً ، يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ الْمَلائِكَةَ لا بُشْرى يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُجْرِمِينَ وَيَقُولُونَ حِجْراً مَحْجُوراً» — *“Those who do not expect to encounter Us say, ‘Why have the angels not been sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?’ Certainly they are too proud of themselves and have become terribly defiant. The day they see the angels, there will be no good news for the guilty on that day, and they will say, ‘Keep off!’”* (24:21-22). This is the day of death or what follows it, as evidenced by His subsequent saying: «أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَيْرٌ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَأَحْسَنُ مَقِيلاً» — *“The inhabitants of paradise will be in a better abode on that day and a better resting place.”* (25:24).

He, the Exalted, says afterward—and the apparent context suggests it is another day: «وَيَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ السَّماءُ بِالْغَمامِ وَنُزِّلَ الْمَلائِكَةُ ، تَنْزِيلاً الْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْحَقُّ لِلرَّحْمنِ وَكانَ يَوْماً عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ عَسِيراً» — *“The day when the sky is rent asunder with clouds and the angels are sent down in a majestic descent. On that day true sovereignty will belong to the All-beneficent, and it will be a hard day for the faithless.”* (25:25-26). Perhaps this is what they meant by their saying: «أَوْ تَأْتِيَ بِاللهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ قَبِيلاً» — *“or bring Allah and the angels face to face [with us].”* (17:92).

In short, His saying, the Exalted: «وَلَوْ أَنْزَلْنا مَلَكاً لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ» etc., is a response to their suggestion that an angel descend to punish them. Based on this, one should add what Allah promised this nation—that He would delay the punishment for them, as indicated by the verses in Surah Yunus: «وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولٌ فَإِذا جاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْقِسْطِ وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ ، وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ، قُلْ لا أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِي ضَرًّا وَلا نَفْعاً إِلَّا ما شاءَ اللهُ لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ» — *“There is an apostle for every nation; so when their apostle comes, judgement is made between them with justice, and they are not wronged. They say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?’ Say, ‘I have no power to benefit or harm myself, except what Allah wills. There is a time for every nation...’”* until He said: «وَيَسْتَنْبِئُونَكَ أَحَقٌّ هُوَ قُلْ إِي وَرَبِّي إِنَّهُ لَحَقٌّ وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ» — *“They ask you to inform them: ‘Is it true?’ Say, ‘Yes! By my Lord, it is indeed true, and you cannot frustrate [Him].’”* (10:47-53). There are many other verses regarding this meaning, which we shall explore fully in another Surah, if Allah wills.

And He, the Exalted, says: «وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنْتَ فِيهِمْ وَما كانَ اللهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ» — *“But Allah would not punish them while you were among them, nor will Allah punish them while they plead for forgiveness.”* (8:33).

The conclusion from the verse is that they ask for the descent of an angel, but We do not grant what they ask, because if the angel were to descend, the matter would be decided between them and they would not be granted respite. Allah has willed to grant them respite for a time, so let them indulge in what they indulge in until they meet their day; and what they asked for will surely come to them, and Allah will judge between them.

It is possible to establish the meaning of the verse in another way: that their intention was for the angel to descend as a sign (آية), not to bring punishment. The meaning of the response would then be that if an angel were to descend upon them, they would not believe in him due to the vice of obstinacy (العناد) and arrogance (الاستكبار) firmly established within them. At that point, the matter would be decided between them and they would not be granted respite, and they do not desire that.

**The second:** That the angel should descend upon them to carry the burdens of the message (الرسالة) and call to Allah in place of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), or be a messenger like him, confirming his call and witnessing to his truthfulness. This is as in their saying, which Allah relates: «وَقالُوا ما لِهذَا الرَّسُولِ يَأْكُلُ الطَّعامَ وَيَمْشِي فِي الْأَسْواقِ لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ فَيَكُونَ مَعَهُ نَذِيراً» — *“And they say, ‘What sort of apostle is this who eats food and walks in the marketplaces? Why has not an angel been sent down to him to be a warner along with him?’”* (25:7). They mean that one who is a messenger from Allah's side should not, in his status, share in the common habits of people like eating food and earning a living by walking in the markets. Rather, he should be distinguished by a heavenly life and a celestial existence, unmixed with the toil of striving and the hardship of material life, so that he may be manifest in his call; or a heavenly angel should descend with him to be a warner with him, so that there remains no doubt regarding the truth of his call and the reality of his message.

This is what the following verse responds to: «وَلَوْ جَعَلْناهُ مَلَكاً» etc.

**His saying, the Exalted:** «وَلَوْ جَعَلْناهُ مَلَكاً لَجَعَلْناهُ رَجُلاً وَلَلَبَسْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ما يَلْبِسُونَ» — *“Had We made him an angel, We would surely have made him a man, and We would have surely confused for them what they are [themselves] confusing.”* (6:9). *Al-Labs* (اللبس) with a *fatha* [on the *lam*] means covering with a veil that which must be covered due to its ugliness or its need for that. *Al-Labs* (اللبس) with a *damma* means obscuring the truth (التغطية على الحق). It seems the meaning is metaphorical, and the root is the same.

Al-Raghib said in *al-Mufradat*: "He wore the garment (لبس الثوب)" means he covered himself with it, and "he clothed another (ألبسه غيره)"... until he said: "The root of *al-lubs* (with a *damma* on the *lam*) is covering a thing. This is said regarding abstract meanings; it is said: 'I confused his matter for him (لبست عليه أمره).'" He said: «وَلَلَبَسْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ما يَلْبِسُونَ» — *“and We would have surely confused for them what they are [themselves] confusing”* (6:9), and He said: «وَلا تَلْبِسُوا الْحَقَّ بِالْباطِلِ» — *“Do not mix the truth with falsehood”* (2:42), «لِمَ تَلْبِسُونَ الْحَقَّ بِالْباطِلِ» — *“Why do you mix the truth with falsehood”* (3:71), «الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا إِيمانَهُمْ بِظُلْمٍ» — *“Those who have faith and do not sully their faith with wrongdoing”* (6:82). And it is said: "In the matter there is *lubsa* (لبسة)," meaning confusion (التباس).

The object of "they confuse" (يلبسون) is omitted. Perhaps from this, generality (العموم) is inferred, and the estimation is: the disbelievers confuse for themselves, which is broader than some confusing for themselves and some confusing for others.

As for their confusing for others, it is like when evil scholars mix the truth with falsehood for their ignorant followers, or when tyrants who are followed mix the truth with falsehood for their weak followers. This is like the saying of Pharaoh, which Allah relates to his people: «يا قَوْمِ أَلَيْسَ لِي مُلْكُ مِصْرَ وَهذِهِ الْأَنْهارُ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِي أَفَلا تُبْصِرُونَ أَمْ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِنْ هذَا الَّذِي هُوَ مَهِينٌ وَلا يَكادُ يُبِينُ ، فَلَوْ لا أُلْقِيَ عَلَيْهِ أَسْوِرَةٌ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ أَوْ جاءَ مَعَهُ الْمَلائِكَةُ مُقْتَرِنِينَ ، فَاسْتَخَفَّ قَوْمَهُ فَأَطاعُوهُ» — *“‘O my people! Does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me and these rivers that flow at my feet? Do you not perceive? Am I not better than this one who is a lowly wretch and can hardly speak clearly? Why have not gold bracelets been cast upon him, or angels come with him in procession?’ So he fooled his people, and they obeyed him.”* (43:51-54). And his saying: «ما أُرِيكُمْ إِلَّا ما أَرى وَما أَهْدِيكُمْ إِلَّا سَبِيلَ الرَّشادِ» — *“I only show you what I see, and I only guide you to the way of rectitude.”* (40:29).

As for their confusing for themselves, it is by imagining to themselves that the truth is falsehood and that falsehood is truth, and then persisting in falsehood. For although man distinguishes truth from falsehood by the primordial nature (الفطرة) — *the innate disposition toward truth that Allah placed in every human being* — and his soul is inspired with its vice and its godwariness, his strengthening of the side of whim (الهوى) and his support for the spirit of lust and anger within himself generates in his soul the habit of arrogance toward the truth and superiority over reality. Thus, his soul becomes attracted to it, deceived by its own deed, and does not allow him to heed the truth or listen to its call. At that point, his deed is made alluring to him, and he mixes the truth with falsehood while he knows, as He, the Exalted, says: «أَفَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلهَهُ هَواهُ وَأَضَلَّهُ اللهُ عَلى عِلْمٍ وَخَتَمَ عَلى سَمْعِهِ وَقَلْبِهِ وَجَعَلَ عَلى بَصَرِهِ غِشاوَةً» — *“Have you seen him who has taken his desire to be his god and whom Allah has led astray knowingly, and set a seal upon his hearing and his heart, and put a blindfold on his sight?”* (45:23). And He said: «قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِالْأَخْسَرِينَ أَعْمالاً الَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُونَ صُنْعاً» — *“Say, ‘Shall we inform you who are the biggest losers in their works? Those whose endeavours go awry in the life of the world, while they suppose they are doing good.’”* (18:103-104).

This is what justifies the depiction of a human being's straying in a matter despite his knowledge of it. Thus, the objection does not hold that a person's confusing the truth with falsehood for himself is an undertaking of certain harm, which is irrational.

Furthermore, if we were to look deeply into the states of our own souls and then act with fairness, we would find evil habits whose badness we acknowledge, yet we do not abandon them due to the rootedness of the habit. This is nothing but straying despite knowledge, confusing the truth with falsehood for the soul, and being distracted by imaginary pleasure and infatuated with it away from remaining firm upon the truth and acting by it. May Allah help us toward His pleasure.

In any case, His saying, the Exalted: «وَلَوْ جَعَلْناهُ مَلَكاً لَجَعَلْناهُ رَجُلاً» etc., is the response to their request for the descent of an angel to be a warner so that they might believe in him.

The gist of it is that this world is the realm of choice (الاختيار), in which man's true felicity is not achieved except by his following the path of choice, and his acquiring for himself or against himself what benefits him in his felicity or harms him. Whichever of the two paths he chooses for himself, Allah, the Glorified, carries that out for him.

He, the Exalted, says: «إِنَّا هَدَيْناهُ السَّبِيلَ إِمَّا شاكِراً وَإِمَّا كَفُوراً» — *“Indeed We have guided him to the way, be he grateful or ungrateful.”* (76:3). It is only guidance and showing the way so that he may choose what he chooses for himself of following the path or rebelling, without being compelled to either of the two paths or forced to follow it. Rather, he sows for himself and then reaps what he sowed. He, the Exalted, says: «وَأَنْ لَيْسَ لِلْإِنْسانِ إِلَّا ما سَعى وَأَنَّ سَعْيَهُ سَوْفَ يُرى ثُمَّ يُجْزاهُ الْجَزاءَ الْأَوْفى» — *“and that nothing belongs to man except what he strives for, and that he will soon be shown his endeavour, then he will be rewarded for it with the fullest reward.”* (53:39-41). Thus, man has nothing but the consequence of his striving; if it is good, Allah shows him that, and if it is evil, He carries it out for him. He, the Exalted, says: «مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الْآخِرَةِ نَزِدْ لَهُ فِي حَرْثِهِ وَمَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الدُّنْيا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْها وَما لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ نَصِيبٍ» — *“Whoever desires the tillage of the Hereafter, We will enhance his tillage for him; and whoever desires the tillage of the world, We will give him [some] of it, and he will have no share in the Hereafter.”* (42:20).

In short, this divine call cannot be established unless it is placed upon human choice without compulsion or forcing. Therefore, it is inevitable that the Messenger carrying the messages of Allah be one of the people, speaking to them in their language, so that they may choose for themselves felicity through obedience or wretchedness through opposition and disobedience, without Allah forcing them to accept the call by a heavenly sign that compels them to it, even though He is capable of that. As He said: «لَعَلَّكَ باخِعٌ نَفْسَكَ أَلَّا يَكُونُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ ، إِنْ نَشَأْ نُنَزِّلْ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ آيَةً فَظَلَّتْ أَعْناقُهُمْ لَها خاضِعِينَ» — *“You might kill yourself [with grief] that they will not believe. If We wish, We will send down to them a sign from the sky before which their heads will remain bowed in humility.”* (26:3-4).

So if Allah were to send down to them an angel as a messenger, it would be a requirement of wisdom (الحكمة) to make him a man like them. Thus, the winners would win by their acquisition, and the losers would lose by confusing the truth with falsehood for themselves and for their followers, just as they confuse it with the human Messenger. Then Allah would carry that out and confuse for them as they confused. He, the Exalted, says: «فَلَمَّا زاغُوا أَزاغَ اللهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ» — *“So when they swerved, Allah made their hearts swerve.”* (61:5).

Thus, the descent of an angel as a messenger would not result in any benefit or effect greater than what results from sending a human messenger; it would then be futile. So the saying of those who disbelieve: «لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ» is nothing but a request for a futile matter that would not result in any specific new effect as they hoped. This is the meaning of His saying: «وَلَلَبَسْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ما يَلْبِسُونَ» — *“and We would have surely confused for them what they are [themselves] confusing.”* (6:9).

From the preceding explanation, several points have become clear:

**First:** That the necessary connection between making the Messenger an angel and making him a man is only from the perspective of the requirement of wisdom to preserve human choice in the divine religious call. For if the angel were sent in his heavenly form, turning the Unseen into the manifest, it would be a form of compulsion (الإلجاء) with which the elective call (الدعوة الاختيارية) could not be established.

**Second:** That what the verse indicates is the angel becoming a man, while remaining silent as to whether this is by the transformation of the angelic essence (الماهية الملكية) into the human essence—which many researchers consider impossible—or by his appearing in a human likeness (التمثيل), like the Spirit appearing to Mary as a well-proportioned human, and the noble angels appearing to Abraham and Lot (peace be upon them) in the form of human guests.

Although most verses regarding angels support the second of the two views, His saying, the Exalted: «وَلَوْ نَشاءُ لَجَعَلْنا مِنْكُمْ مَلائِكَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ يَخْلُفُونَ» — *“Had We wished, We would have made angels from among you to be [Our] successors on the earth”* (43:60) is not devoid of some indication toward the first view. This discussion has further details that should be sought elsewhere.

**Third:** That His saying, the Exalted: «وَلَلَبَسْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ما يَلْبِسُونَ» is of the same nature as His saying: «فَلَمَّا زاغُوا أَزاغَ اللهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ» — *“So when they swerved, Allah made their hearts swerve.”* (61:5). Thus, it is a divine leading astray (إضلال) of them after they preferred straying for themselves, without it being an initial leading astray, which would be unbecoming of His holy presence, the Glorified.

**Fourth:** That the omitted object of "they confuse" (يلبسون) is general, including their confusing for themselves and the confusion of some of them for others.

**Fifth:** That the gist of the verse is an argument against them: that if an angel were sent to them with the message, it would not benefit them in removing their confusion. For Allah would then make the angel a man resembling the human Messenger, and they would confuse for themselves with him and be skeptical. They only desire by this request to be rid of the human Messenger who is in the form of a man, in order to replace their doubt with certainty; but if the angel were to become of this description—as he inevitably must—they would not benefit from that at all.

**Sixth:** That in the expression: «لَجَعَلْناهُ رَجُلاً» — *“We would surely have made him a man”*, and He did not say: "We would have made him a human (بشراً)" to include both man and woman, there is an indication—as has been said—that the Messenger can only be a man. Similarly, the expression is not devoid of an indication that this making is by the appearance of the angel in the form of a human, rather than the transformation of his identity into the identity of a human, as has been said.

Most commentators have explained the verse by saying the meaning is: since they could not endure seeing the angel in his original form due to their immersion in the material world, if an angel were sent to them, it would be necessary for him to appear to them as a well-proportioned human. At that point, there would appear to them the same confusion and doubt that appears with a messenger from among humans, and they would not benefit from it at all.

This explanation is insufficient for the soundness of the response, even if we grant that an ordinary human cannot witness angels in their original forms based on verses like His saying: «يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ الْمَلائِكَةَ لا بُشْرى يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُجْرِمِينَ» — *“The day they see the angels, there will be no good news for the guilty on that day.”* (25:22).

This is because if witnessing the angel in his original form were impossible for a human, the state of human individuals would not differ in terms of possibility and impossibility. Yet it has been narrated in the reports of both groups [Sunni and Shi'a] that the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) saw Gabriel in his original form twice. It is within the power of Allah to strengthen other people for what He strengthened His Prophet for, so they might behold them and believe in them. There is no hindrance to this according to wisdom except the hindrance of compulsion (الإلجاء); that is the hindrance that must be refuted by the verse, as mentioned before.

Similarly, witnessing the angel in the form of humans does not necessitate the possibility of doubt and confusion. For Allah, the Glorified, informs regarding Abraham and Lot (peace be upon them) that they beheld the angels in the form of humans, then recognized them and did not doubt their matter. Likewise, He informed regarding Mary that she witnessed the Spirit, then recognized him and did not doubt him, nor was his matter confused for her. Why then would it not be possible for the state of other people to be like their state (peace be upon them) in beholding the angel in the form of a human, then recognizing him and having certainty in his matter? Were it not that making the souls of all people like the souls of Abraham, Lot, and Mary would necessitate the erasure of their instincts and primordial natures and their replacement with pure, holy souls—and in that is the hindrance of compulsion. Thus, compulsion is the hindrance with which the subject of trial (الامتحان) would no longer remain, and it is what must be refuted by the verse, as mentioned before.

**His saying, the Exalted:** «وَلَقَدِ اسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ» — *“Apostles were certainly derided before you...”* (6:10) until the end of the two verses. *Al-Hayq* (الحيق) means descending and befalling. In the *Mufradat* of al-Raghib: It is said its root is *haqqa* (حق), so it was inverted like *zalla* (زل) and *zala* (زال). It has been recited: «فَأَزَلَّهُمَا الشَّيْطانُ» — *“Then Satan caused them to slip”* (2:36) and *fa-azalahuma* (فأزالهما) [caused them to move away]. Based on this are *dhammahu* (ذمه) and *dhamahu* (ذامه).

Their mockery of the messengers was by mocking the punishment they were warned would descend and befall them. Thus, the very thing they mocked befell them. In the first verse, there is a solace for the soul of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and a warning to the polytheists; and in the second verse, a command to take heed and an exhortation.
